summary: McKenzie discovers he failed to upload the correct installer for Bingo Card Creator after making major changes. The deployment process was manual, and he missed the step of copying the new build to the web directory before syncing to the FTP server. As a result, all users had been downloading the old version for days. He takes the mistake in stride rather than dwelling on it.
key_points: Manual deployment processes are error-prone and can lead to shipping the wrong build
A missed step in a multi-step manual workflow went unnoticed for days
Early-stage solo software businesses often lack deployment automation
money_quotes: Unfortunately, this is largely a manual process and I missed the critical "copy the new installer from the build directory to the web directory before sync'ing the local copy of my website and the FTP server".
key_insight: Manual deployment processes without checklists or automation will inevitably lead to shipping the wrong build.
